proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_cetuximab=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_cetuximab=0.
Patients receiving treatment_bevacizumab=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_bevacizumab=0.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_pembrolizumab=0.
Patients receiving treatment_encorafenib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_encorafenib=0.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_trastuzumab_tucatinib=0.
Patients receiving treatment_regorafenib=1 have longer mean pfs_months than those with treatment_regorafenib=0.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.29 on cetuximab vs 4.32 off; difference -0.038 mo, t-test p=0.089. No significant overall benefit.
Mean pfs_months 4.30 on bevacizumab vs 4.32 off; diff -0.019, p=0.354. No significant benefit.
Mean pfs_months 4.32 on pembrolizumab vs 4.31 off; diff +0.005, p=0.855. No effect.
Mean pfs_months 4.32 on encorafenib vs 4.31 off; diff +0.005, p=0.873. No effect.
Mean pfs_months 4.27 on tras+tuc vs 4.32 off; diff -0.044, p=0.246. No effect.
Mean pfs_months 5.09 on regorafenib vs 4.12 off; diff +0.97 mo, p<1e-300. Strongly positive overall effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (each 1-unit increment shortens PFS).
Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
Patients with right_sided_primary=1 have shorter pfs_months than right_sided_primary=0.
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (older patients fare worse, as in most oncology cohorts).

analyses.result_summary: ECOG slope -1.190 mo per unit (PFS 5.27 / 4.05 / 2.91 mo for ECOG 0/1/2); p<1e-300. Strong negative prognostic.
Mean PFS 3.70 (stage IV) vs 5.05 (not stage IV); diff -1.35 mo, p<1e-300. Strong negative prognostic.
Mean PFS 4.11 (right-sided) vs 4.42 (left-sided); diff -0.31 mo, p=5.2e-46. Negative prognostic.

Age slope is +0.176 mo per year (POSITIVE, opposite to expected). PFS rises monotonically from 0.26 mo (age <36) to 8.4 mo (age 84-90). Hypothesis refuted; direction reversed and highly significant (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher cea_ng_ml is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr (neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Refined: older age_years is associated with LONGER pfs_months in this cohort (counterintuitive direction noted in iteration 2).

analyses.result_summary: Albumin slope +0.465 mo per g/dL, p=3e-115. Positive prognostic.
Weight-loss slope -0.073 mo per percent loss, p=2e-167. Strongly negative.
CEA slope -0.0054 mo per ng/mL, p=3e-17. Negative prognostic.
LDH slope -0.00032 mo per U/L, p=0.013. Marginally negative; effect strengthens after multivariable adjustment (-0.0005, p<1e-300 of 8.9 |t|).
CRP slope -0.0020 mo per mg/L, p=0.065. Trend toward negative; not significant unadjusted.
NLR slope +0.0052 mo per unit, p=0.29. Not significant in either direction; hypothesis refuted.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + cea_ng_ml + weight_loss_pct_6mo', df) and decile means
analyses.result_summary: Confirmed: age slope is +0.176 mo per year and persists after controlling for all major prognostic covariates (p<1e-300). Decile means rise monotonically: ~0.26 mo at <36 yrs to ~8.4 mo at 84-90 yrs. This is a robust positive linear association unique to this synthetic dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in kras_mutation=0 (wild-type) patients than in kras_mutation=1 patients (canonical anti-EGFR predictive biomarker).
Cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in nras_mutation=0 patients than in nras_mutation=1 patients.
Cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e=0 patients than in braf_v600e=1 patients.
Pembrolizumab benefit on pfs_months is larger in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients (canonical immunotherapy predictive biomarker).
Encorafenib benefit on pfs_months is larger in braf_v600e=1 patients than in braf_v600e=0 patients (targeted-therapy predictive biomarker).
Trastuzumab+tucatinib benefit on pfs_months is larger in her2_amplified=1 patients than in her2_amplified=0 patients.
Cetuximab benefit on pfs_months is larger in left-sided (right_sided_primary=0) than in right-sided primaries.

analyses.result_summary: KRAS WT: cetux on=4.42 vs off=4.46 (diff -0.05); KRAS mut: on=4.10 vs off=4.13 (diff -0.03). Interaction +0.020, p=0.665. Hypothesis refuted: cetuximab has no detectable benefit in either KRAS subgroup.
Cetuximab x NRAS interaction -0.007, p=0.96. No predictive signal.
Cetuximab x BRAF interaction -0.026, p=0.81. Refuted.
MSI-H: pembro on=4.30 vs off=4.29 (diff +0.007); MSS: diff +0.005. Interaction +0.002, p=0.99. Strongly refuted: pembrolizumab has no benefit even in MSI-H patients.
BRAF V600E: enco on=3.98 vs off=4.11 (diff -0.13, NS); BRAF WT: diff +0.01. Interaction -0.141, p=0.38. Refuted.
HER2 amplified: tras+tuc on=4.27 vs off=4.26 (diff +0.02); HER2 negative: diff -0.05. Interaction +0.063, p=0.78. Refuted.
Left-sided: cetux on-off diff -0.05; right-sided: -0.02. Interaction +0.026, p=0.58. No sidedness modification of cetuximab effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, sidedness, labs, and treatments, kras_mutation=1 and braf_v600e=1 are independently associated with shorter pfs_months (i.e., they act prognostically rather than predictively in this cohort).
After multivariable adjustment, treatment_regorafenib remains the only treatment with a significant main effect on pfs_months; the other five treatments are null.

analyses.result_summary: In an MV model with no treatment-x-biomarker interactions, KRAS coef=-0.354 (p<1e-300), BRAF V600E coef=-0.357 (p=1e-79). Hypothesis supported at this stage. NRAS, MSI-H, HER2 amplified all null.

Adjusted treatment coefficients (95%CI): cetuximab -0.011 (p=0.19), bevacizumab +0.003 (p=0.73), pembrolizumab 0.0001 (p=0.99), encorafenib +0.006 (p=0.63), tras+tuc +0.026 (p=0.07 trend), regorafenib +0.943 (p<1e-300). Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined: regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in kras_mutation=0 patients and absent or markedly attenuated in kras_mutation=1 patients (i.e., KRAS is a predictive biomarker for regorafenib).
Refined: regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in braf_v600e=0 patients and absent in braf_v600e=1 patients.
Regorafenib's PFS benefit varies by nras_mutation status.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib x KRAS interaction coef -1.629, p<1e-200. KRAS WT: regorafenib on=5.74 vs off=4.12 (+1.66 mo). KRAS mut: regorafenib on=4.15 vs off=4.13 (+0.03 mo, NS). Strong predictive interaction supports the hypothesis.
Regorafenib x BRAF V600E interaction -1.183, p=6e-23. BRAF WT: rego +1.03 mo; BRAF V600E: rego -0.16 (no benefit). Predictive interaction confirmed.

Regorafenib x NRAS interaction +0.988, p=1.2e-11. NRAS-mutant patients (who, by mutual exclusivity with KRAS/BRAF, are typically RAS/RAF WT for KRAS+BRAF) appear to derive larger regorafenib benefit. After adjusting for KRAS and BRAF interactions jointly, the NRAS-specific interaction shrinks to +0.04, p=0.46 (non-significant), indicating the apparent NRAS effect is mediated by KRAS/BRAF status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining rasraf_wt = kras_mutation==0 AND nras_mutation==0 AND braf_v600e==0, regorafenib produces substantially larger PFS gains in rasraf_wt=1 patients than in rasraf_wt=0 patients.

Within the rasraf_wt=1 subgroup, the other five treatments (cetuximab, bevacizumab, pembrolizumab, encorafenib, trastuzumab+tucatinib) still show no benefit, leaving regorafenib as the only effective drug in this subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: RAS/RAF WT (n=25,310, 50.6%): regorafenib on=5.92 vs off=4.12 (+1.81 mo). RAS/RAF mutant: rego on=4.25 vs off=4.12 (+0.13 mo, p=1e-4 but small). Interaction +1.678, p<1e-240. Hypothesis strongly supported.

analyses.code: ttests for each treatment within df[df.rasraf_wt==1]
analyses.result_summary: In RAS/RAF WT: cetuximab diff -0.05 (p=0.17); bevacizumab -0.003 (p=0.93); pembrolizumab -0.008 (p=0.84); encorafenib +0.034 (p=0.50); tras+tuc -0.067 (p=0.24); regorafenib +1.81 (p<1e-300). Supported: only regorafenib benefits this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Regorafenib's PFS benefit is concentrated in left-sided primaries (right_sided_primary=0); right-sided patients gain little or none.

The largest regorafenib PFS gains occur in patients who are simultaneously kras_mutation=0 AND right_sided_primary=0; the 3-way interaction is significantly positive (rescuing some right-sided KRAS-mutant patients), but the dominant pattern is that regorafenib helps almost exclusively in left-sided KRAS WT.
In the doubly-selected subgroup of left-sided AND RAS/RAF WT patients, the regorafenib PFS gain exceeds 2.5 months even after multivariable adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Regorafenib x right-sided interaction -1.454, p<1e-160. Left-sided: rego +1.48 mo; right-sided: rego +0.03 mo (NS). Hypothesis supported.

Three-way interaction +2.348, p<1e-100. Left-sided KRAS WT: regorafenib +2.52 mo; right-sided KRAS WT: rego +0.07 (NS); left-sided KRAS mut: rego +0.06 (NS); right-sided KRAS mut: rego +0.07. Regorafenib benefit is concentrated almost entirely in left-sided KRAS WT, as expected.

analyses.code: ols on subset df[(rasraf_wt==1)&(right_sided_primary==0)]: 'pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + cea_ng_ml + ldh_u_l'
analyses.result_summary: n=16,443 (3,264 regorafenib-treated). Adjusted regorafenib coef = +2.73 mo, p<1e-300. Hypothesis supported with large magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Once treatment_regorafenib x kras_mutation, x braf_v600e, and x right_sided_primary interactions are included in a multivariable model, the apparent prognostic main effects of kras_mutation, braf_v600e, and right_sided_primary largely disappear, indicating that those biomarkers are not independently prognostic but rather identify patients in whom regorafenib is ineffective.
Pembrolizumab benefit on PFS is larger in MSI-H patients with low-burden disease (e.g., stage_iv=0) or low ECOG, which the simple interaction missed.
Cetuximab benefit emerges in the left-sided RAS/RAF WT subgroup that mirrors the canonical clinical population.
Concurrent treatment_encorafenib AND treatment_cetuximab in BRAF V600E patients gives a synergistic PFS benefit (BEACON-style combination).

analyses.code: smf.ols(big formula with rego x kras, rego x braf, rego x right_sided interactions), R^2=0.898
analyses.result_summary: After including the three regorafenib-x-biomarker interactions, the main-effect coefficients become: kras_mutation -0.001 (p=0.92), braf_v600e +0.003 (p=0.85), right_sided_primary +0.003 (p=0.67). All three lose significance, while regorafenib main effect grows to +2.29 (p<1e-300). Hypothesis supported: KRAS, BRAF V600E, and right-sided primary are not independent prognostic factors; their apparent prognostic effects are explained by regorafenib's lack of efficacy in those groups.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * msi_high * stage_iv', df) and pembro x ecog
analyses.result_summary: All pembrolizumab interaction terms with msi_high, stage_iv, ECOG, and tp53 mutation are non-significant (p>0.1 for each). Hypothesis refuted: no rescue subgroup found for pembrolizumab.

n=5,067 cetux-treated vs 11,376 untreated within left-sided RAS/RAF WT. Mean PFS 4.62 vs 4.68; diff -0.07, p=0.13. Hypothesis refuted: cetuximab has no detectable benefit even in the canonical responder subgroup.
In BRAF V600E (n=2,272): cetux only diff -0.10; enco only diff -0.21; combo diff +0.01. Combo interaction +0.32, p=0.32. Hypothesis refuted: no synergy detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: One or more snp_* polymorphisms modify regorafenib efficacy; specifically, snp_rs1799983 is hypothesized to enhance regorafenib benefit (positive interaction).
Comorbidities (diabetes_mellitus, hypertension, copd, chronic_kidney_disease, heart_failure) shorten pfs_months independently.
Race/ethnicity, insurance type, rural residence, and education_years are associated with pfs_months (disparities hypothesis).
tp53_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, pten_loss, cdkn2a_loss, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, and ntrk_fusion each have main prognostic effects on pfs_months.

Final check: the +0.18 mo/year age slope is not driven by any subset and is not an artefact of treatment selection (the slope persists within every treatment arm and within the regorafenib-x-RAS/RAF subgroups).

analyses.code: for each snp_*: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_regorafenib * snp_X', df)
analyses.result_summary: Across 26 SNPs the smallest p-values for regorafenib-x-SNP interactions are p=0.008 (rs1799983 +0.14), p=0.022 (rs1128503 -0.12), p=0.022 (rs3813867 -0.26), p=0.027 (rs1801133 +0.12), p=0.040 (rs1800470 +0.10). None survive a Bonferroni threshold of 0.05/26=0.0019. The directionally-positive rs1799983 interaction (p=0.008) is the closest to significant but does not survive correction. Hypothesis at best weakly supported and likely a multiple-testing artefact.

analyses.code: ttests for 15 comorbidity columns vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All p>0.05 (smallest: depression_anxiety p=0.05 with diff -0.05; atrial_fib p=0.12). Hypothesis refuted: comorbidities do not predict PFS in this cohort.

analyses.code: ANOVA / OLS for race_ethnicity, insurance_type, rural_residence, education_years
analyses.result_summary: race_ethnicity F-test p=0.48; insurance_type F-test p=0.22; rural_residence p=0.34; education_years p=0.34. Hypothesis refuted: no sociodemographic disparity detected.

analyses.code: ttests for tp53, pik3ca, pten_loss, cdkn2a_loss, her2_amplification, met_exon14_skipping, ret_fusion, ros1_fusion, nrg1_fusion, fgfr_alteration, ntrk_fusion, keap1
analyses.result_summary: All p>0.15 (smallest tp53 p=0.38, her2_amplification p=0.24). Hypothesis refuted: none of these additional molecular features have main effects on PFS.

analyses.code: stratified slope: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years', df) within each treatment arm and within rasraf_wt x right_sided strata
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted age slope = +0.176 mo/year (p<1e-300) in the comprehensive multivariable model, and +0.176 mo/year (p<1e-300) in a model that further controls for KRAS, BRAF, sidedness, and regorafenib x biomarker interactions (R^2 0.898). The coefficient is stable across all stratifications. Robustness confirmed: positive linear age-PFS association is a hallmark feature of this synthetic cohort.
